outlines.content:
# MZ-2026-08-01 — The Last Light Of Summer

Logline: At MonkeyZoo’s end-of-summer night festival, NeonBlue races after a moving blackout pattern and must choose overlooked people over the public spotlight when a cyan pulse echoes his guilt about Patch.
Theme: Honest hope can admit danger
Page count: 8
Emotional arc: Cheerful over-reassurance → fear dressed as slogans → honest plan → hope that includes risk
Conflict: Moving power failures pull NeonBlue into denial while Lil Devil pushes for reckless force; the team must rescue a group trapped in a dark service corridor during the main-stage countdown
Ending: Festival continues reduced; NeonBlue refuses to call the night perfect; a partial cyan Echo segment flickers unseen

## Page map
Page 1 — Arrival and over-volunteering at the festival grounds
Page 2 — First blackout and failed backup reassurance
Page 3 — Outages travel; fractured cyan on public screens; Static hears a pattern
Page 4 — Lil Devil at the service gate and control box; system reacts to NeonBlue
Page 5 — Midpoint: Patch-linked frequency marker; Moodz names the pretending
Page 6 — Crisis: overlooked group trapped in festival service corridor vs main stage countdown
Page 7 — Climax: honest plan; directed force; rescue of the left-behind
Page 8 — Resolution and partial Echo reveal; new question about the relay

## Cast
- Featured: NeonBlue (MZ-CHAR-005)
- Guest: Lil Devil (MZ-CHAR-LILDEVIL)
- Supporting: Static, TwoTone, Scarline, Moodz, Ash

## Locations (approved canon)
- festival-grounds, festival-main-stage, festival-service-corridor, festival-control-node

## Props (approved canon)
- public-projection-screen, festival-backup-panel, service-gate, control-box, cyan-relay-marker, echo-symbol

## Continuity notes
- Proposed story canon: Patch emotional link; cyan relay memory; do not resolve Patch status.
- Echo: one-sixth of symbol lights only after NeonBlue chooses the unseen group.

panels.dialogue: NEONBLUE: We can cover games, rides, and the stage—easy!
panels.caption: End of summer, Zoo City festival lights

panels.dialogue: LIL DEVIL: Or we force the fun.
panels.caption: Volunteering without consent
panels.continuity_notes: Lil Devil restless energy; NeonBlue cheerful

panels.dialogue: NEONBLUE: Backup systems never miss a finale.
panels.caption: Public spotlight warming up
panels.continuity_notes: Main stage empty of crisis yet

panels.dialogue: STATIC: Something’s wrong in the power.
panels.caption: First failure

panels.dialogue: NEONBLUE: It’s fine—watch the backup kick!
panels.caption: Reassurance before facts

panels.dialogue: SCARLINE: Panic isn’t the real threat.
panels.caption: Backup fails

panels.dialogue: TWOTONE: Two routes converging.
panels.caption: Pattern emerges

panels.dialogue: STATIC: It’s not noise—it’s a pattern.
panels.caption: Cyan fracture on screens
panels.continuity_notes: Fractured cyan motif; not full Echo

panels.dialogue: NEONBLUE: We’ve got this—everyone stay happy!
panels.caption: Optimism as armor

panels.dialogue: LIL DEVIL: Smash the box. Open it.
panels.caption: Impatience at the gate

panels.dialogue: NEONBLUE: Wait—it changed when I got near.
panels.caption: System reacts to NeonBlue
panels.continuity_notes: Key beat: different reaction near NeonBlue

panels.dialogue: ASH: Hope can read warnings.
panels.caption: Ash line
panels.continuity_notes: Proposed Ash line; not a forced catchphrase brand

panels.dialogue: NEONBLUE: I’ve seen this pulse before…
panels.caption: Midpoint recognition
panels.continuity_notes: Patch link emotional only; no full explain

panels.dialogue: MOODZ: You don’t have to pretend this feels safe.
panels.caption: Moodz truth

panels.dialogue: TWOTONE: Stage is the distraction path.
panels.caption: Converging data

panels.dialogue: NEONBLUE: If we lose the stage, the whole festival panics—
panels.caption: Public pressure

panels.dialogue: STATIC: They’re stuck back here!
panels.caption: Overlooked trap

panels.dialogue: NEONBLUE: …They’ll be left behind.
panels.caption: Choice forming

panels.dialogue: NEONBLUE: The system may fail. Here’s what we do anyway.
panels.caption: Honest plan

panels.dialogue: LIL DEVIL: Fine—directed smash only.
panels.caption: Restrained power

panels.dialogue: NEONBLUE: We get everyone out. That’s the win.
panels.caption: Rescue of the left-behind

panels.dialogue: NEONBLUE: Not perfect. We got them out. That’s enough.
panels.caption: Reduced celebration

panels.dialogue: NEONBLUE: Why did that relay know me?
panels.caption: New question

outlines.content:
# MZ-2026-09-02 — Signals In The Silence

Logline: After summer, storm-season devices all emit the same interference; Static proves the noise is a structured six-tone search while Clever maps the hardware, and Static learns to share one clear warning at a time.
Theme: Sensitivity without being silenced
Page count: 8
Emotional arc: Overload and dismissal risk → structured proof → choice to keep listening on his terms → clear usable warning
Conflict: Public systems broadcast interference Static alone can parse; overload makes him hard to hear even when he is right
Ending: Old relay junction located before overload; Static’s four-part method for future emergencies

## Page map
Page 1 — Tiny disruptions only Static notices
Page 2 — Storm burst hits every device; Static reacts first
Page 3 — Clever inspects equipment; Static over-explains
Page 4 — Six tones isolated; one matches August cyan frequency
Page 5 — Signal searching for responses, not broadcasting outward
Page 6 — Emergency systems catch interference; team gives Static space
Page 7 — Static reduces to one repeating element; points to old relay junction
Page 8 — Method established; powered-off radio soft tone final image

## Cast
- Featured: Static (MZ-CHAR-003)
- Guest: Clever Monkey (MZ-CHAR-CLEVER)
- Supporting: NeonBlue, TwoTone, Moodz, Scarline, Ash

## Locations (approved canon)
- storm-routines, transit-announcement-hub, school-pa-zone, old-relay-junction, zoo-city-streets

## Props (approved canon)
- transit-board, school-speaker, personal-device-burst, signal-analyzer, six-tone-motif, powered-off-radio, cyan-relay-marker, echo-symbol

## Continuity notes
- Six tones include August cyan frequency match
- Do not treat Static as irrational
- Growth is method, not a cure

panels.action: One tone matches August’s cyan frequency; NeonBlue goes still.
panels.dialogue: STATIC: That’s the cyan one… from summer.
panels.caption: August frequency match
panels.visual_notes: MonkeyZoo house style; early-fall storm light; keep Static identity readable

panels.action: TwoTone helps pair frequencies into readable couples.
panels.dialogue: TWOTONE: Pair them. Don’t stack them.
panels.caption: Paired frequencies
panels.visual_notes: MonkeyZoo house style; early-fall storm light; keep Static identity readable

panels.location: Early-Fall Storm Streets and Routine Nodes
panels.action: Static realizes the signal is searching for responses, not broadcasting out.
panels.dialogue: STATIC: It’s listening for us.
panels.caption: Searching not broadcasting
panels.visual_notes: MonkeyZoo house style; early-fall storm light; keep Static identity readable

panels.action: Moodz blocks pressure for Static to “just calm down.”
panels.dialogue: MOODZ: He doesn’t have to be comfortable for you.
panels.caption: Protection from pressure
panels.visual_notes: MonkeyZoo house style; early-fall storm light; keep Static identity readable

panels.action: Scarline offers a clear choice: keep listening or stop.
panels.dialogue: SCARLINE: Do you want to keep listening?
panels.caption: Clear choice
panels.visual_notes: MonkeyZoo house style; early-fall storm light; keep Static identity readable

panels.location: Early-Fall Storm Streets and Routine Nodes
panels.action: Interference spreads into emergency systems; Static begins repeating every danger at once.
panels.dialogue: STATIC: Flood—fire—relay—stop—
panels.caption: Overload crisis
panels.visual_notes: MonkeyZoo house style; early-fall storm light; keep Static identity readable

panels.action: Team gives Static space instead of ordering silence.
panels.dialogue: MOODZ: Room. Not volume.
panels.caption: Space not order
panels.visual_notes: MonkeyZoo house style; early-fall storm light; keep Static identity readable

panels.action: Static answers Scarline: yes—on his terms.
panels.dialogue: STATIC: Yes. But one line at a time.
panels.caption: Choice yes
panels.visual_notes: MonkeyZoo house style; early-fall storm light; keep Static identity readable

panels.action: Static reduces the interference to one repeating element and states it cleanly.
panels.dialogue: STATIC: The third tone. Every twelve seconds. West sector.
panels.caption: One clear warning
panels.visual_notes: MonkeyZoo house style; early-fall storm light; keep Static identity readable

panels.action: Team reaches the old relay junction before overload; cyan residual glow in dormant nodes.
panels.dialogue: CLEVER: Junction confirmed. Acting now.
panels.caption: Relay located
panels.visual_notes: MonkeyZoo house style; early-fall storm light; keep Static identity readable

panels.action: Team acts on Static’s warning; systems stabilize enough for a breath.
panels.dialogue: NEONBLUE: We heard you.
panels.caption: Warning used
panels.visual_notes: MonkeyZoo house style; early-fall storm light; keep Static identity readable

panels.action: Static writes his method: sense / know / fear / check.
panels.dialogue: STATIC: What I sense. What I know. What I fear. What needs checking.
panels.caption: Method established
panels.visual_notes: MonkeyZoo house style; early-fall storm light; keep Static identity readable

panels.location: Early-Fall Storm Streets and Routine Nodes
panels.action: Clever packs the analyzer; respects Static’s emotional reading as data of another kind.
panels.dialogue: CLEVER: I can map the how. You map the why.
panels.caption: Guest resolution
panels.visual_notes: MonkeyZoo house style; early-fall storm light; keep Static identity readable

panels.action: Final image: powered-off radio still emits one soft final tone.
panels.caption: Final soft tone
panels.visual_notes: MonkeyZoo house style; early-fall storm light; keep Static identity readable

outlines.content:
# MZ-2026-10-02 — The House That Remembers

Logline: MonkeyZoo reopens a haunted attraction over old FusionZoo infrastructure; Scarline recognizes danger others treat as theater while Zombie confirms residual past systems, and Scarline must guide without controlling.
Theme: Experience can guide without controlling
Page count: 8
Emotional arc: Detached calm read as secrecy → shared risk → informed choice → belief in validation over rescue
Conflict: Misdirect exits and fear-responsive rooms; one door must stay closed
Ending: Group chooses with eyes open; Scarline's origin is a real choice she was given, not a scar; partial season signal continues

## Page map
Page 1 — Playful attraction scares; Scarline unimpressed
Page 2 — Zombie recognizes old-system residue / smell
Page 3 — Room changes; exit signs disagree
Page 4 — Scarline names the one door that must not open
Page 5 — Midpoint: Scarline admits she has been here before
Page 6 — False rescue choices; Zombie past vs Scarline present
Page 7 — Crisis: guided choice, not orders
Page 8 — Land; residual wall mark; season signal thread warm

## Cast
- Featured: Scarline (MZ-CHAR-006)
- Guest: Zombie Monkey (MZ-CHAR-ZOMBIE)
- Supporting: Static, Moodz, TwoTone, NeonBlue, Ash

## Locations (approved / proposed)
- haunted-attraction (proposed if not yet filed), abandoned FusionZoo path residue, exit corridors

## Props
- misdirect-exit-sign, wall-marking-residue, theatrical scare props vs real residue

## Continuity notes
- Signal belongs to older FusionZoo system (Echo mystery tracker Issue 03)
- No facial scar; green glow only if zombie/cracked-chamber intentional
- Do not complete Echo emblem
